framework_items.principle_statement: Loan origination models are stronger when the lender retains meaningful economic risk after origination because incentives remain tied to long-term loan performance.
framework_items.normalized_principle: In lending, retained risk is a major signal of underwriting quality.
metric_definition: retained_risk: economic exposure the lender keeps after originating loans.
loan_performance: realized repayment and loss performance of originated loans.
credit_quality: underlying ability of borrowers to repay.
metric_usage_context: retained_risk: used to judge whether incentives remain aligned after origination.
loan_performance: used to assess whether underwriting quality is genuine.
credit_quality: used to distinguish real underwriting from fee-driven volume.
metric_limitations: retained_risk: can be nominal rather than meaningful if exposure is trivial.
performance_logic: When originators keep risk, fee incentives are less able to overwhelm credit discipline, improving loan quality over time.
evaluation_questions: Does the lender keep meaningful exposure after origination?
Are underwriting incentives aligned with long-term loan performance?
Is credit quality driven by real repayment ability or by securitization demand?
diagnostic_signals: Lender exposure retained after origination.
Loan quality tied to repayment ability rather than packaging demand.
practical_rules: Treat skin in the game as a key alignment test in lending.
Discount fee-heavy origination models that offload risk immediately.
example_buffett_phrasing: Keep skin in the game

framework_items.subsection: Underwrite for reality, not for packaging
framework_items.principle_statement: Financial engineering and complex structuring can hide poor underlying economics, so analysis should focus on understandable assets, aligned incentives, and true spread economics.
framework_items.normalized_principle: Prefer transparent finance with understandable risk and retained economic logic.
metric_definition: underlying_asset_quality: the real credit and cash-flow quality of the financed asset.
spread_economics: profitability earned from the true difference between funding cost and asset yield after losses.
structured_finance_risk: risk hidden or redistributed through complex packaging.
metric_usage_context: underlying_asset_quality: used to see whether the asset makes sense without heroic analysis.
spread_economics: used to judge whether profitability comes from real economics rather than risk transfer.
structured_finance_risk: used to test whether complexity is disguising weak underwriting.
metric_limitations: structured_finance_risk: packaging can obscure poor economics and misaligned incentives.
spread_economics: can be overstated if losses are transferred temporarily rather than eliminated.
performance_logic: Transparent lending models are more reliable because profits come from understandable spread economics instead of from selling risk to someone else.
evaluation_questions: Is the underlying asset understandable without heroic analysis?
Are incentives aligned across originator, packager, and ultimate risk-bearer?
Is apparent profitability coming from spread economics or from selling risk to someone else?
diagnostic_signals: Profitability dependent on packaging or distribution rather than loan performance.
Complex structures that obscure who ultimately bears the risk.
practical_rules: Prefer understandable lending economics over engineered complexity.
Trace profits back to the real asset and the real risk-bearer.
example_buffett_phrasing: Underwrite for reality, not for packaging

framework_items.subsection: Match funding structure to balance-sheet realities
framework_items.principle_statement: Borrowing short and lending long can be dangerous unless natural hedges, liquidity reserves, or broader balance-sheet offsets protect the enterprise.
framework_items.normalized_principle: Analyze funding structure at the consolidated economic level, not only at the subsidiary level.
metric_definition: asset_duration: time profile over which the firm's assets repay or reprice.
liability_duration: time profile over which liabilities mature or reprice.
liquidity_reserves: funds available to bridge funding stress.
interest_rate_sensitivity: exposure of earnings or value to changes in rates.
metric_usage_context: asset_duration: used to test maturity mismatch against liabilities.
liability_duration: used to assess refinancing or repricing risk.
liquidity_reserves: used to judge whether mismatches are survivable.
interest_rate_sensitivity: used to identify whether the enterprise is asset-sensitive or liability-sensitive.
metric_limitations: asset_duration: maturity mismatch can look manageable until markets tighten.
liquidity_reserves: headline liquidity may not be enough if funding markets freeze.
performance_logic: Funding structure becomes dangerous when liability timing and asset timing diverge without adequate reserves or natural offsets.
evaluation_questions: Is the company borrowing short and lending long?
What natural offsets or liquidity reserves protect against rate shocks?
Is the enterprise asset-sensitive or liability-sensitive?
diagnostic_signals: Large maturity mismatch without strong reserves.
Heavy dependence on market access to refinance short liabilities.
practical_rules: Analyze funding risk on a consolidated economic basis.
Require liquidity or natural hedges when maturity mismatch exists.
example_buffett_phrasing: Match funding structure to balance-sheet realities

framework_items.subsection: Treat all shareholders equally
framework_items.principle_statement: Management should reject selective disclosure and communicate important information equally to all owners rather than favoring institutions or analysts.
framework_items.normalized_principle: Communication style is evidence of governance quality and shareholder alignment.
performance_logic: When management communicates equally and owner-to-owner, it reinforces trust and alignment; selective disclosure suggests a market-theater mentality rather than partnership.
evaluation_questions: Does management provide equal access to important information?
Is communication designed for owners or for market theater?
Does management explain the business as if speaking to intelligent long-term partners?
diagnostic_signals: Equal access to material information.
Owner-oriented communication rather than selective access.
practical_rules: Use disclosure practices as a governance signal.
Prefer managers who treat all shareholders as equal partners.
example_buffett_phrasing: Treat all shareholders equally

framework_items.subsection: Report as you would wish to be reported to
framework_items.principle_statement: Useful disclosure should be candid, decision-useful, and framed as if management were reporting to itself as a long-term owner.
framework_items.normalized_principle: Owner-oriented reporting is a meaningful mark of management quality.
performance_logic: Reporting quality matters because investors allocate capital based on what management chooses to explain, omit, and normalize; reciprocal, owner-oriented reporting reduces informational gamesmanship.
evaluation_questions: Is disclosure genuinely useful for understanding economics?
Does the company explain segment economics in a decision-useful way?
Are adjustments and judgments explained candidly?
diagnostic_signals: Candid explanation of segment economics and judgments.
Disclosure designed to help owners understand economics rather than theater.
practical_rules: Judge reporting by whether it would satisfy you if roles were reversed.
Prefer candid, decision-useful disclosure over promotional presentation.
example_buffett_phrasing: Report as you would wish to be reported to
